title: Registrars in Goa cannot treat Indian civil court decrees as ‘foreign’ under Portugese CPC: Bombay High Court
description: The Bombay High Court recently ruled that a decree passed by a competent civil court in India cannot be construed as a foreign decree under the Portuguese Code of Civil Procedure, 1939. A Division Bench of Justices Valmiki Menezes and Amit Jamsandekar directed the State Registrar and all sub-registrars in Goa not to treat the decrees issued by any civil court in India as foreign decrees.The Court also said that the authorities cannot insist on review and confirmation by the High Court for d
